question: In the 2018 VSCode blog post on replit.com, what was the command they clicked on in the last video to remove extra lines?

research_queries: replit.com VSCode 2018 remove extra lines command
working_plan: Search for the specific 2018 VSCode blog post on replit.com
Access and examine the blog post content
Locate the video(s) mentioned in the post
Find the 'last video' referenced in the question
Identify the specific command clicked to remove extra lines
Extract the exact command name for the answer

solver.reasoning_summary: Found the July 1, 2018 VSCode blog post 'Zero Setup VSCode Intelligence' on replit.com which demonstrated Language Server Protocol features including formatting. The post lists formatting as one of the demonstrated features. In VSCode 2018, 'Format Document' was the standard command used for formatting that would remove extra blank lines as part of the formatting process.

issues: The reasoning does not provide direct evidence from watching the video itself - it infers what command might be used for formatting rather than citing what was actually clicked in 'the last video'
The question specifically asks 'what was the command they clicked on in the last video' which requires direct observation of video content, not general knowledge about VSCode formatting commands
No description of actually viewing the video or seeing the specific command that was clicked
